title: Writing: Present Actions and Future Plans
learningGoal: Construct sentences using the present continuous and simple future tenses, demonstrating accurate gender agreement.
instructions: In this writing task, you must assume the identity of a MALE speaker. You will translate sentences describing what you are doing right now, and what you will do later.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi (Assume you are a MALE speaker):
1. I am drinking water right now. (Drink = Pee)
2. I will go to Delhi tomorrow. (Go = Jaa)
3. What are you doing? (Formal 'Aap', Do = Kar)
4. Please come tomorrow. (Polite future command of 'Aana')

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: मैं अभी पानी पी रहा हूँ। कल मैं दिल्ली जाऊँगा। आप क्या कर रहे हैं? कल आइयेगा।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Main abhi paani pee raha hoon. Kal main Delhi jaunga. Aap kya kar rahe hain? Kal aaiyega.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: I am drinking water right now. Tomorrow I will go to Delhi. What are you doing? Please come tomorrow.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect masculine grammar. Uses 'raha hoon' and 'jaunga'. Correctly uses the polite future request 'aaiyega'.

evaluationCriteria.description: Must use 'raha' in sentence 1 and 'unga' in sentence 2.
Must use 'rahe hain' with 'Aap' in sentence 3.
Must attach 'ga' to the formal imperative 'Aaiye' resulting in 'Aaiyega'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: मैं अभी पानी पी रहा हूँ। कल मैं दिल्ली जाऊँगा। आप क्या कर रहे हैं? कल आइयेगा।
acceptableAlternates: Main abhi paani pee raha hoon. Kal main Delhi jaunga. Aap kya kar rahe hain? Kal aaiyega.
main abhi paani pee raha hoon. kal main delhi jaunga. aap kya kar rahe hain? kal aaiyega.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict check on male endings.

criteria.name: Gender Failure Check
criteria.description: If user writes 'pee rahi hoon' or 'jaungi', score is 0. They must assume a male identity.

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic! Your tense formations and gender agreements are spot on.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the instructions carefully. You must write as a MALE speaker ('raha' and 'unga').
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Check your formal request. To say 'Please come tomorrow', you take the polite 'Aaiye' and add the future 'ga' -> 'Aaiyega'.

hintText: Pay attention to sentence 3. 'Aap' (Formal You) always takes the plural forms: 'rahe' and 'hain'.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Aap kya kar raha hai' (Mixing formal Aap with singular raha/hai).
